summary: Cleaned up src/melee/ft/ft_0BEC.c by replacing several large inline ASM fallback blocks with C implementations for death-state-related fighter setup functions. The PR reused already matched logic as a basis, factored repeated fighter initialization into setupInitialState, added small fighter-specific helper inlines for Fox and Dr. Mario item creation/scaling, and retained explicit MUST_MATCH unused stack padding where needed. The author noted the file had some awkward fakematch behavior around setupInitialState, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, GET_FIGHTER versus direct user_data access, and stack/codegen differences.

key_files.role: Only changed file; converted/cleaned matched fighter death-state helper functions and removed large ASM fallback blocks.

what_changed: Removed file-local LITERAL float constants ftCo_804D8C00, ftCo_804D8C04, ftCo_804D8C08, and ftCo_804D8C0C in favor of direct float literals such as 0.f, 1.f, 0.71428f, and 0.8f in the C code.
Converted ftCo_800BECB0 from an ASM/MUST_MATCH guarded implementation into a normal C function.
Converted ftCo_800BED88 from an ASM/MUST_MATCH guarded implementation into a normal C function.
Converted ftCo_800BEF04 from an ASM/MUST_MATCH guarded implementation into a normal C function.
Introduced static inline setupInitialState(Fighter* fp) to centralize repeated initialization of x2219_b2, x2219_b1, x2228_b1, item_gobj, and x1984_heldItemSpec.
Kept explicit unused stack padding under MUST_MATCH in ftCo_800BECB0 and ftCo_800BED88, changing the local padding style to int unused[2].
Changed motion-state calls to use named enum constants where visible in the diff, including ftCo_MS_DeadDown, ftCo_MS_DeadRight, and ftCo_MS_DeadUpStarIce, with explicit float arguments.

Added static inline FoxHelper(Fighter_GObj* gobj, Fighter* fp) to create Fox's blaster gun item through it_802AE994, using ftParts_8007500C(fp, FtPart_RThumbNb), da->x20_FOX_BLASTER_GUN_ITKIND, and scaling via it_8026BAE8.
Added static inline DocHelper(Fighter_GObj* gobj, Fighter* fp) to create Dr. Mario vitamin item objects via it_802C09C4 and apply 0.71428f-scaled model size.
Preserved empty stubs ftCo_800BED84, ftCo_800BEF00, and ftCo_800BEFD0.

For ftCo_800BED88, the implementation uses Fighter* fp = gobj->user_data rather than GET_FIGHTER(gobj), matching the author's note that GET_FIGHTER versus implicit/direct access affected generated code.
smart_moves: Factored repeated post-motion-state initialization into a static inline helper, reducing duplicate decomp code while still allowing the compiler to inline it for matching-sensitive codegen.
Split Fox and Dr. Mario item setup into small static inline helpers, making the switch cases much shorter while preserving the exact item creation and scaling operations.
Used named motion-state constants instead of raw numeric literals where possible, improving readability without changing behavior.
Retained MUST_MATCH-only stack padding to account for required unused stack behavior instead of deleting matching scaffolding entirely.
Used direct gobj->user_data in ftCo_800BED88 where the PR body says GET_FIGHTER introduced an unwanted addi instruction, showing a practical codegen-driven workaround.
decomp_lessons: When multiple matched functions share identical bitfield and pointer initialization, a static inline helper can improve maintainability while still matching, but inlining and parameter choices must be checked against generated assembly.

GET_FIGHTER and direct gobj->user_data access may not always compile identically; if a macro or cast changes register allocation or inserts an addi, direct access can be a viable matching tactic when evidence supports it.
Replacing raw motion-state IDs with enum names is useful cleanup once the values are known, but the resulting call codegen must still be verified.
Local unused stack arrays remain a valid matching tool for functions whose original stack frame has unexplained padding.
Removing inline ASM blocks after producing a matching C implementation substantially improves future maintainability and searchability.
Small helper inlines can isolate character-specific item setup patterns, such as Fox blaster, Dr. Mario vitamin, and Ness item creation, while keeping shared death-state setup centralized.
naming_conventions: Common fighter functions in this file use the ftCo_800Bxxxx address-based naming convention, e.g. ftCo_800BECB0, ftCo_800BED88, and ftCo_800BEF04.
New internal helpers use descriptive CamelCase-ish names in the diff: setupInitialState, FoxHelper, and DocHelper.
Fighter motion-state constants use ftCo_MS_* names, such as ftCo_MS_DeadDown, ftCo_MS_DeadRight, and ftCo_MS_DeadUpStarIce.
Fighter kind checks use FTKIND_* constants, including FTKIND_FOX, FTKIND_DRMARIO, and FTKIND_NESS.
Struct fields retain existing decomp placeholder/member naming such as x2219_b2, x2219_b1, x2228_b1, x1984_heldItemSpec, x34_scale, co_attrs, model_scaling, cur_pos, and facing_dir.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed large nofralloc ASM fallback sections after matching C implementations were available.
Used static inline helpers instead of out-of-line helpers to preserve likely original inline behavior and avoid extra calls.
Maintained MUST_MATCH-only unused stack padding with int unused[2] in functions that still needed stack-frame adjustment.
Used explicit float literals in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, e.g. 0.f, 1.f, and 0.f, to match floating-point argument generation.
Used gobj->user_data instead of GET_FIGHTER in ftCo_800BED88 because the author observed GET_FIGHTER or pointer handling could introduce an unwanted addi instruction compared with decomp.me.

The author explicitly characterized part of the solution as a fakematch around setupInitialState and Fighter_ChangeMotionState interactions; future work should treat that area as matching-sensitive rather than proven source-accurate.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.

The PR body contains author self-review noting that some matches may be fakematches and that setupInitialState likely should include Fighter_ChangeMotionState, but doing so caused codegen differences.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for ft_0BEC.c to see whether the author-noted fakematches around setupInitialState, GET_FIGHTER, and gobj->user_data were resolved.
Search for setupInitialState or similar shared fighter death-state initialization helpers to verify whether the naming or factoring survived later cleanup.
Search for other PRs replacing ASM nofralloc blocks with C in fighter code while retaining MUST_MATCH unused stack padding.
Search for GET_FIGHTER codegen issues or comments mentioning unwanted addi instructions in matched functions.
